mnemonics: Pandas are SHAPELY: Series (1D), Head/Info/Describe (initial look), Attributes (.shape, .dtypes), Pandas (the library), Loc/Iloc (selection), Export (to_csv), and Your data is now a DataFrame (2D).
keyPoints: A Series is a one-dimensional array (a single column), while a DataFrame is a two-dimensional table (multiple columns).
Use .loc for label-based indexing and .iloc for integer position-based indexing to select data.
Methods like .head(), .info(), and .describe() provide a quick, high-level overview of your dataset's structure and summary statistics.
commonMistakes: Forgetting that in-place operations (like df.drop()) often require the `inplace=True` parameter or reassignment (df = df.drop()).
Using square brackets [] for multiple column selection without a list, e.g., df['col1', 'col2'] instead of df[['col1', 'col2']].
